category_title: Students with autism spectrum disorder who shift conversational topics to personal interests
minimal_context: Jeff is participating in a language-dependent social task within a cooperative learning group.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Jeff, please share your ideas with your cooperative learning group for this activity.

STUDENT: (Sits quietly with the peers and does not volunteer information or ask questions.)

TUTOR: Go ahead and tell them what you think.

STUDENT: The sheeps went... (Offers a conversational bid, but the others in the group ignore his contribution.)

TUTOR: Keep trying to talk to your group.

STUDENT: (Remains passively unengaged, does not answer questions, and does not initiate any further conversations with the group.)

options.A: Award points at the end of the activity during brief private meetings with Jeff in an instructive and encouraging manner.
options.B: Create seating arrangements so that Jeff is always part of a cooperative learning group, and rotate him among different peer groups.
options.C: Front-load by coaching Jeff before group work, modeling the form for a question and answer (e.g., 'What idea do you have?' and 'I think we should...').
options.D: Deliver twelve hour-long sessions, scheduled twice-weekly, of a structured mindfulness intervention program to support Jeff during cooperative learning.
